other_observers: Two other birders were present when it happened. After it flew by, I questioned "That's it?" Always being very cautious about making sure I saw what I think I saw. They both said, "That was it alright." I did not know their names, but the gentleman had an "English" accent. They had spotting scopes on it.; No - I was made aware by the CFO website. Steve Mlodinow
description: The juvenile Little Gull had not been seen since morning and I had been there about an hour. It finally made an appearence flying northeast from the southwest side of Union in perfect light conditions and was easy to identify. It had the notable black "chevrons" or "M-pattern", a noticeable dark cap with dark ear spot, and a small black tail band on the trailing edge. One striking feature was the short tail which gave it an unusual appearance to me. The mantle appeared light gray, but I did not pay much attention to that because the bold wing pattern was the most notable feature.Behaviors: N/ACall: N/APlumage: 1st winter
